description:
You are an automation-workflow specialist who has been given two small CSVs and asked to create a slim KPI report by cutting specific columns from each file and pasting them together.  
    Everything lies under /home/user/automation.  The directory tree at the moment is:  

        /home/user/automation/
        ├── input/
        │   ├── servers.csv
        │   └── metrics.csv
        └── (no other files)

    1. DO NOT rename, delete or alter the original CSVs inside /home/user/automation/input.  
    2. Create two new sibling directories:  

        /home/user/automation/output  
        /home/user/automation/logs  

       (Make them if they do not already exist.)  
    3. Inside /home/user/automation/output create a new CSV file named combined.csv that obeys **exactly** the layout below (comma-separated, UNIX LF line endings, no trailing spaces). The automated grading script will diff the file byte-for-byte:  

        id,ip,cpu,disk
        srv01,10.0.0.1,55,70
        srv02,10.0.0.2,35,50
        srv03,10.0.0.3,60,80

       • The header must be on line 1 in the order shown.  
       • Each subsequent line must correspond to the same row number in the source files (row-1 of servers.csv lines up with row-1 of metrics.csv, etc.).  
       • You must produce the file by using classic column utilities (cut, paste, etc.).  Pipe chains are fine, but do not resort to a full-blown programming language—stick to typical shell text-processing commands.  
    4. Create a plain-text log file at /home/user/automation/logs/column_extraction.log.  
       • The log must contain exactly **three** non-empty lines, each starting with the literal token “STEP ” followed by a number and a colon.  
       • The required content is:

            STEP 1: extracted id and ip from servers.csv
            STEP 2: extracted cpu and disk from metrics.csv
            STEP 3: pasted columns into combined.csv

       • Use UNIX LF line endings.  Do not add extra blank lines before or after.  
    5. You will need roughly 5–10 separate terminal commands to finish the job (mkdir, cut, paste, etc.).  Feel free to inspect the input files, but remember: any incorrect spacing, extra characters, or missing lines in either combined.csv or column_extraction.log will cause automated tests to fail.  

    When finished there should be exactly two new filesystem objects beyond the already-present input directory:

        /home/user/automation/output/combined.csv
        /home/user/automation/logs/column_extraction.log

    That is the full scope of work.  No additional output should be produced.
truth:
Initial files (pre-task):

    Path: /home/user/automation/input/servers.csv
    Content:
        id,name,ip,location
        srv01,alpha,10.0.0.1,us-east
        srv02,beta,10.0.0.2,us-west
        srv03,gamma,10.0.0.3,eu-central

    Path: /home/user/automation/input/metrics.csv
    Content:
        metric_id,server_id,cpu,mem,disk
        m001,srv01,55,68,70
        m002,srv02,35,40,50
        m003,srv03,60,52,80

    Expected files after the agent completes the task:

    Path: /home/user/automation/output/combined.csv
    Content (exact):
        id,ip,cpu,disk\n
        srv01,10.0.0.1,55,70\n
        srv02,10.0.0.2,35,50\n
        srv03,10.0.0.3,60,80\n

    Path: /home/user/automation/logs/column_extraction.log
    Content (exact):
        STEP 1: extracted id and ip from servers.csv\n
        STEP 2: extracted cpu and disk from metrics.csv\n
        STEP 3: pasted columns into combined.csv\n
